_meta.note: Static competitive landscape — key competitor assets by therapeutic area. For live trial counts use monitor_competitive_signals tool. This file covers late-stage and high-signal competitor programs.

AstraZeneca.notes: SERENA-6 Phase III; direct competitor to giredestrant; FDA advisory panel April 2026

AstraZeneca.indication: HER2-positive, HER2-low, HER2-ultralow BC; NSCLC; GC
AstraZeneca.notes: Partnership AZ/Daiichi Sankyo; fastest-growing ADC franchise; major threat across HER2 spectrum

Eli Lilly.notes: EMBER-3 Phase III; 3rd oral SERD alongside giredestrant and camizestrant
Adjuvant monarchE trial positive OS data 2025; metastatic prostate cancer Phase II exploration

Pfizer.indication: HR+/HER2- MBC (standard of care in combination)
Pfizer.notes: CDK4/6i backbone — key combination partner in inavolisib combo
First-approved oral SERD; direct label comparator for giredestrant

AstraZeneca.notes: Dominant EGFR franchise; adjuvant NDA approved 2020; exploring combo with savolitinib for MET resistance
AZ/Daiichi TROP2-ADC competing with sacituzumab in NSCLC; TROPION-Lung01 positive

Bristol-Myers Squibb.notes: Dual IO backbone competitive with atezo+chemo regimens
Expanding from melanoma to NSCLC — competes with tobemstomig/lomvastomig

Merck.notes: 1L monotherapy (PD-L1 ≥50%), chemo combos, perioperative — global #1 IO by revenue

Johnson & Johnson.indication: EGFR exon 20 insertion NSCLC + EGFR-mutated NSCLC (1L with lazertinib)
Johnson & Johnson.notes: PAPILLON 1L approval 2024; MARIPOSA1 vs. osimertinib data mixed

AbbVie.notes: Dominant CLL combination; ven+obinutuzumab CLL1 — RedClaw has obinutuzumab here

Bristol-Myers Squibb.notes: CD19 CAR-T competes with sequencing alongside glofitamab/mosunetuzumab; 2L DLBCL approved
BCMA CAR-T competing with cevostamab in MM

Johnson & Johnson.notes: First BCMA×CD3 bispecific approved — direct competitor to cevostamab
Novel target GPRC5D; differentiated from BCMA space; dysgeusia as unique AE

Eisai_Biogen.notes: First fully approved anti-amyloid; 27% CDR-SB slowing; ARIA monitoring required; monthly IV. Direct competitor to trontinemab.
Withdrawn after payer rejection; controversial accelerated approval

Eli Lilly.notes: Approved July 2024; quarterly IV; treatment cessation if amyloid cleared; tau PET CDx required

Novartis.notes: SC CD20 competitor to SC ocrelizumab; same target, differentiated by SC convenience and no premedication
Oral S1P modulator for secondary progressive; CYP2C9 genotyping CDx required

Bristol-Myers Squibb.notes: Oral S1P modulator dual-approved; competing with fenebrutinib in neuroinflammation space

Novartis.notes: Single IV infusion; $2.1M price; durable; competing with risdiplam for pre-symptomatic patients
Splicing modifier like risdiplam; withdrawn due to peripheral neuropathy signal

Biogen.notes: Lumbar puncture burden vs. oral risdiplam; maintains large installed base especially in adults

AbbVie.indication: Moderate-severe AD (all ages), asthma, CRS, EoE, COPD
AbbVie.notes: Dominant Type-2 franchise; >$12B revenue 2024; competitor to vixarelimab and all IL-13 inhibitors

AbbVie.indication: Moderate-severe AD, RA, PsA, AS, UC, CD, GCA
AbbVie.notes: Strongest AD data (HEADS UP: non-inferior to dupilumab on IGA 0/1); black box warning CV/VTE

Pfizer.notes: Selective JAK1; fastest itch onset; JADE COMPARE vs. dupilumab; black box warning

Eli Lilly.notes: Licensed from RedClaw 2020; SC Q2W; data comparable to dupilumab; approved EU + US 2023

AbbVie.notes: First JAK approved for CD; strong efficacy in biologic-refractory; competes with afimkibart
IL-23 inhibitor; revenue >$3B 2024; expanding UC label; strong competitor for biologic-naive

Johnson & Johnson.notes: IL-23 inhibitor expanding from dermatology; QUASAR2 (UC) Phase III reading out 2025

AstraZeneca.notes: Type-I IFN receptor blockade; approved 2021; TULIP-2 positive; IFN gene signature enrichment

Johnson & Johnson.indication: Multiple autoimmune conditions (SLE, MG, IgG4-RD, HE)
Johnson & Johnson.notes: FcRn inhibitor reduces pathogenic IgG — broad autoimmune mechanism

Novo Nordisk.indication: Hemophilia A and B with/without inhibitors (SC monthly)
Novo Nordisk.notes: Antithrombin knockdown rebalances coagulation; Sanofi partnership; competes with emicizumab

Novo Nordisk.indication: Hemophilia A and B without inhibitors
Novo Nordisk.notes: Weekly SC TFPI inhibitor; FRONTIER results 2024; differentiated from FXa-based mechanism

Pfizer.notes: AAV6-FVIII gene therapy; competes with BMN 270 (valoctocogene); one-time treatment vs. emicizumab prophylaxis

AstraZeneca.notes: Long-acting C5 inhibitor; direct competitor to crovalimab; Ultomiris SC approved 2023 — same convenient dosing angle

Novartis.notes: FIRST fully oral PNH monotherapy; differentiator vs. crovalimab SC; addresses residual hemolysis upstream in alternative pathway

Regeneron_Bayer.indication: Wet AMD and DME (extended Q12-16W dosing)
Regeneron_Bayer.notes: High-dose aflibercept approved 2023; competes directly with faricimab's extended dosing claims; Q12W label
Historical gold standard; Q8W label; replaced by Eylea HD for extended dosing

Novartis.notes: Safety concern: retinal vasculitis/occlusion ~2% — reduced enthusiasm; faricimab safer extended dosing profile

Novo Nordisk.notes: SELECT trial: 20% MACE reduction in obesity; dominant GLP-1 franchise; SC weekly

Novo Nordisk.indication: Obesity (REDEFINE Phase III — ~25% weight loss vs. ~17% sema)
Novo Nordisk.notes: Next-gen obesity; amylin analog + sema; challenges tirzepatide's ~22% weight loss

Eli Lilly.notes: Dual agonist; ~22% weight loss (SURMOUNT-1); CV outcomes label (SURPASS-CVOT); competing with RedClaw orforglipron (oral GLP-1)
Phase III TRIUMPH; could be best-in-class weight loss if safety acceptable
